title: Leak-safe circuit-specific hazard priors from prior seasons
summary: Official F1 strategy interviews describe teams using previous years, historical degradation, and pre-race simulations to estimate likely stop counts and windows.
rationale: Official F1 strategy interviews describe teams using previous years, historical degradation, and pre-race simulations to estimate likely stop counts and windows. The dataset spans repeated races across four years, so smoothed prior-year hazard tables should be a strong signal if built temporally and fold-safely.
implementation_hint: For each fold, compute prior-only tables such as `P(PitNextLap=1 | Race, Compound, Stint, TyreLife_bin, RaceProgress_bin)` and `expected laps until next stop` using earlier seasons or excluded-race data only. Feed these priors as features into the existing model rather than using pooled all-years target encoding.
expected_effect: Provides strong circuit-specific stop-window priors for tracks with repeatable strategy patterns, while staying simpler than full external joins.
risk: Only four seasons are available and regulations shifted across 2022-2025, so pooled priors can mislead unless they are time-aware and heavily smoothed.
